_meta.purpose: Per-state capital-gains treatment for a future state layer on the Phase 11 investment calculators. The federal calculators ship WITHOUT this layer; it turns on only when this file is signed off and a state layer is built.
_meta.status: RESEARCHED 2026-07 — awaiting owner sign-off. Not yet used by the site.
_meta.rule: No figure enters this file without a source URL on a state government / revenue-department domain. A null is a correct, expected value; a guessed number is not.
_meta.researchNote: Every non-ordinary jurisdiction (special / reduced_rate / excluded / none) was document-verified against a state government source. The ~34 plain 'ordinary' jurisdictions cite each state's DOR individual-income-tax landing page (they conform to federal taxable income and tax gains at ordinary rates) rather than a deep-linked confirmation per state.

schema.treatment: 'ordinary' | 'excluded' | 'special' | 'reduced_rate' | 'none' | null. ordinary = taxed at the state's normal income rate; excluded = a % of long-term gain is exempt; special = a standalone excise distinct from income tax (Washington); reduced_rate = a preferential flat rate replaces the ordinary rate on long-term gain (Hawaii, Montana); none = no state tax on the gain.
schema.ordinaryRateApplies: boolean|null — true when gains are taxed at the state's ordinary income rate.
schema.ltcgExclusionPct: number|null — fraction of LONG-term gain excluded (0..1). Only when treatment='excluded'.
schema.specialRate: number|null — the flat rate when treatment='special' or 'reduced_rate'.
schema.specialRateThreshold: number|null — the gain amount above which specialRate applies (Washington's annual exemption).
schema.source: string|null — URL on a state government / revenue-department domain.

reviewerFlags: MO: 2025 HB 594 may exempt individual capital gains entirely — marked 'ordinary' (confidence medium); confirm effective date/scope, could be 'none' for 2026.
NM: 40% exclusion is business-sale-only since Jan 1 2025 — marked 'ordinary' for general portfolio gains (confidence medium); confirm 2026 PIT-1/PIT-B.
WA: specialRateThreshold ($278,000) is the 2025 exemption; the 2026 inflation-adjusted figure was not yet published. WA also adds 2.9% over $1M (combined 9.9%).
HI and MT are reduced-RATE states (preferential flat rate), classified 'reduced_rate' rather than 'special' (WA-style excise) or 'excluded'.
Ordinary-state sources are DOR landing pages, not per-state deep-linked confirmations.

AL.note: Capital gains taxed as ordinary income (top rate 5%).

AK.note: No state personal income tax; capital gains untaxed.

AZ.note: Flat 2.5% income tax applies to capital gains as ordinary income. A prior 25% long-term-gain subtraction was repealed with the move to the flat tax.

AR.note: 50% of net capital gain excluded; net capital gain above $10,000,000 is fully excluded (only first $10M taxed). Short-term gains taxed as ordinary income.

CA.note: No preferential rate; gains taxed as ordinary income (up to 13.3% incl. 1% mental-health surcharge over $1M).

CO.note: Flat 4.4% on gains as ordinary income. A narrow subtraction survives only for certain Colorado agricultural land held long-term; not for general portfolio gains.

CT.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income under the graduated tax.

DE.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income (top rate 6.6%).

FL.note: No state personal income tax; capital gains untaxed.

GA.note: Flat 5.19% (2025) income tax applies to gains as ordinary income.

HI.note: Reduced RATE, not a standalone excise. Alternative-tax computation caps NET capital gain at a maximum 7.25% rate (HRS 235-51/235-71); taxpayer pays lower of ordinary rates or 7.25%. Short-term gains taxed at ordinary rates up to 11%.

ID.note: Flat 5.3% (2025) on gains as ordinary income. A 60% deduction exists ONLY for qualifying Idaho real/tangible property; not general stock/portfolio gains.

IL.note: Flat 4.95% on gains as ordinary income; no preferential treatment.

IN.note: Flat state rate (~3.0%, 2025) on gains as ordinary income; plus county rates.

IA.note: Flat 3.8% (2025) on gains as ordinary income. A capital gains deduction survives only for qualifying farm/business assets; not general portfolio gains.

KS.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income under graduated rates.

KY.note: Flat 4.0% (2025) on gains as ordinary income.

LA.note: Flat 3.0% (2025) on gains as ordinary income. An exclusion exists only for net gain from the sale of a Louisiana business; not general portfolio gains.

ME.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income (top rate 7.15%).

MD.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income; plus local (county) income tax. A 2025 surcharge on high-income investment income applies at the top.

MA.note: Long-term gains taxed at 5% (the ordinary MA rate). BUT short-term gains taxed at 8.5% and collectibles at 12% (with 50% deduction). Additional 4% surtax on taxable income over ~$1M.

MI.note: Flat 4.25% on gains as ordinary income; some cities levy local income tax.

MN.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income (top 9.85%); plus a 1% net investment income tax on such income over $1M.

MS.note: Flat 4.4% (2025) on gains as ordinary income. Exemption exists only for gains on stock of certain Mississippi domestic companies.

MO.note: REVIEWER FLAG: Missouri enacted 2025 legislation (HB 594) to exempt individual capital gains. Marked ordinary pending confirmation of effective date/scope; could be 'none' for 2025/2026. Verify against DOR guidance before sign-off.

MT.note: Reduced RATE, not a standalone excise. Net LONG-term gains taxed at 3.0% below a filing-status threshold (single $20,500 / MFJ $41,000 / HoH $30,750) and 4.1% above (vs ordinary 4.7%/5.9%), effective 2024 (HB 221). Replaced Montana's former 30% exclusion. specialRate shown is the top tier. Short-term gains taxed as ordinary income.

NE.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income (~5.2% 2025). A one-time exclusion applies only to gains on an employer corporation's capital stock.

NV.note: No state personal income tax; capital gains untaxed.

NH.note: Interest & Dividends tax repealed effective Jan 1, 2025 (HB 2, 2023). NH never taxed capital gains; capital gains untaxed.

NJ.note: Net gains taxed as ordinary income under graduated rates (top 10.75%). NJ does not allow capital losses to offset other income categories.

NM.note: REVIEWER FLAG: CHANGED effective Jan 1, 2025 (Sec. 7-2-34). General net capital gains now get only a flat deduction up to $2,500; the 40% deduction (on up to $1M) now applies ONLY to the sale of a New Mexico business. So for typical portfolio gains NM is effectively ordinary. Confirm against 2026 PIT-1/PIT-B instructions before sign-off.

NY.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income (top 10.9% incl. surcharges); NYC residents also pay city income tax.

NC.note: Flat 4.25% (2025) on gains as ordinary income.

ND.note: 40% of net long-term capital gain excluded (gain allocable to ND). Short-term gains taxed as ordinary income under ND's low graduated rates (top ~2.5%).

OH.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income (moving toward flat ~2.75%, 2025); some municipalities exempt capital gains from local tax but state taxes them.

OK.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income (top 4.75%). Deduction (68 O.S. 2358) applies only to Oklahoma property or stock of Oklahoma-headquartered companies; not general portfolio gains.

PA.note: Net gains are a taxable class of income at the flat 3.07% rate; no holding-period distinction. Losses in one class cannot offset another.

SC.note: 44% of net capital gain (long-term, holding > 1 yr) deducted (SC Code 12-6-1150). Short-term gains taxed as ordinary income.

SD.note: No state personal income tax; capital gains untaxed.

TN.note: No broad personal income tax; the Hall tax on interest/dividends was fully repealed effective tax year 2021. Capital gains untaxed.

TX.note: No state personal income tax; capital gains untaxed.

UT.note: Flat 4.55% (2025) on gains as ordinary income (a limited credit exists for gains reinvested in Utah small-company stock).

VT.note: Taxpayer elects EITHER a flat $5,000 exclusion OR a 40% exclusion of adjusted net capital gain from assets held > 3 years. The 40% option applies only to certain asset categories, is capped at $350,000, and cannot exceed 40% of federal taxable income. Short-term gains taxed as ordinary income.

VA.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income (top 5.75%). A subtraction exists only for narrowly defined qualified technology business investments.

WA.note: Standalone 7% excise on LONG-term capital gains (RCW 82.87) above an annual standard deduction; PLUS an added 2.9% on gains over $1,000,000 (combined 9.9%), effective tax year 2025 (ESSB 5813). REVIEWER FLAG: specialRateThreshold $278,000 is the 2025 inflation-adjusted exemption; the 2026 figure was not yet published and will be higher. Short-term gains are NOT subject to the tax.

WV.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income under graduated rates (top ~4.8%, 2025).

WI.note: 30% of net long-term capital gain excluded (60% for farm assets). Short-term gains taxed as ordinary income.

WY.note: No state personal income tax; capital gains untaxed.

DC.note: Gains taxed as ordinary income under DC's graduated rates (top 10.75%).
